title: Cross-page landing observability journey into the debugger
description: Verifies a user can go from the overview page into the debugger and inspect a call’s token breakdown and JSON payloads.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Wait for the recent calls section to finish loading
Click a recent call row
Click the navigation link to open the debugger
Select the same call in the debugger timeline or list
Verify a prompt/completion token breakdown is displayed for the selected call
Verify JSON context and response payloads are displayed for the selected call

title: View 30-day overview diagnostics
description: Verifies the overview page loads aggregate usage/cost summary cards, a 30-day token usage chart, and either recent calls or an empty state.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Wait for the overview content to finish loading
Verify aggregate usage and cost summary cards are displayed
Verify a 30-day token usage chart is displayed
Verify a recent calls list is displayed or an empty state is displayed

title: Inspect a call to view token breakdown and JSON payloads
description: Verifies that a user can select a call from the timeline and view token breakdown and JSON context/response payloads for the selected call.

steps.description: Navigate to /debugger
Wait for the call timeline area to finish loading
Click a call in the timeline
Wait for the call details panel to finish loading
Verify prompt and completion token breakdown information is displayed
Verify JSON context and response payloads are displayed

title: Overview diagnostics render with metrics and recent calls list
description: Verifies that the overview page loads 30-day summary metrics, a token trend chart, and the recent calls section.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Wait for the overview metrics section to finish loading
Verify usage/cost/calls summary metrics are displayed
Verify a 30-day token trend chart and a recent calls section are displayed

title: Complete privacy deletion workflow end-to-end
description: Verifies a user can filter memories for a user, delete one memory, then perform GDPR bulk deletion for the same user and see the filtered list become empty.

steps.description: Navigate to /memory
Fill a valid user ID into the user ID filter field
Submit the user ID filter
Select a non-All category chip
Click the delete control for one memory item
Confirm the single-memory deletion
Open the GDPR bulk delete flow
Fill the same user ID into the GDPR user ID field
Confirm the bulk deletion
Verify the memory list shows an empty state for that user ID and selected category after the workflow completes

title: Open a recent call from the overview list
description: Verifies a user can select a recent call from the overview page and view call-related details in the UI.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Wait for the recent calls section to finish loading
Click a recent call row
Verify the selected call’s details view is displayed
Verify call payload information is displayed for the selected call

title: Perform GDPR bulk delete for a user with confirmation
description: Verifies a user can bulk-delete all memories for a given user ID using the GDPR delete flow and the list refreshes afterward.

steps.description: Navigate to /memory
Open the GDPR bulk delete flow
Fill a valid user ID into the GDPR user ID field
Confirm the bulk deletion
Fill the same user ID into the main user ID filter field
Submit the user ID filter
Verify the memory list shows an empty state for that user ID after the bulk delete

title: Filter memories by user ID and category
description: Verifies a user can filter the memory list by developer user ID and then narrow results using category chips.

steps.description: Navigate to /memory
Fill a valid user ID into the user ID filter field
Submit the user ID filter
Select a non-All category chip
Verify the memory list updates to show only memories matching the selected filters or an empty state for those filters

title: View template versions and metadata for a selected prompt
description: Verifies that a user can select a prompt template and view its immutable versions, variables, labels, and timestamps.

steps.description: Navigate to /prompts
Wait for the prompt template list to finish loading
Click a prompt template in the list
Wait for the versions panel to finish loading
Verify a list of prompt versions is displayed for the selected template
Verify version details show content and metadata such as variables, labels, and timestamps

title: Delete a single memory with confirmation
description: Verifies a user can delete an individual memory and the list updates after confirming the deletion.

steps.description: Navigate to /memory
Fill a valid user ID into the user ID filter field
Submit the user ID filter
Click the delete control for one memory item
Confirm the single-memory deletion
Verify the memory list refreshes and the deleted memory item is no longer present

title: Switch between calls and update inspected details
description: Verifies that selecting a different call updates the displayed token breakdown and JSON payloads to match the newly selected call.

steps.description: Navigate to /debugger
Wait for the call timeline area to finish loading
Click a call in the timeline
Wait for the call details panel to finish loading
Click a different call in the timeline
Wait for the call details panel to finish loading
Verify the call details view updates to reflect the newly selected call

title: View sessions list and expand/collapse a session
description: Verifies a user can view the session table (or empty state), expand a session to load messages, read them, and collapse the session.

steps.description: Navigate to /sessions
Wait for the sessions list to finish loading
Click a session row to expand it
Wait for the message history to finish loading
Click the expanded session row to collapse it
Verify user and assistant messages are displayed when expanded
Verify the message history is hidden after collapsing

title: Deploy a non-production prompt version and see deployment state update
description: Verifies that deploying a non-production version updates the UI to reflect the new deployment state for the selected environment.

steps.description: Navigate to /prompts
Wait for the prompt template list to finish loading
Click a prompt template in the list
Wait for the versions panel to finish loading
Click the deploy action for a non-production version
Wait for the deploy operation to complete
Verify the selected version shows an updated deployment label or state indicating it is deployed

title: Analytics charts and cost breakdown table render for selected period
description: Verifies that the analytics page loads summary cards, usage/cost charts, and a cost breakdown table for the current selected period.

steps.description: Navigate to /analytics
Wait for the analytics summary section to finish loading
Scroll to the charts section
Verify daily token usage and cost-by-model charts are displayed
Verify a cost breakdown table with rows is displayed

title: Rollback to a previous eligible version with confirmation
description: Verifies that a user can rollback to a previous eligible version and the UI updates to reflect the rollback result.

steps.description: Navigate to /prompts
Wait for the prompt template list to finish loading
Click a prompt template in the list
Wait for the versions panel to finish loading
Click the rollback action for an eligible version
Confirm the rollback
Verify the deployment state updates to reflect the rolled back version

title: Inspect message history for two different sessions
description: Verifies a user can expand one session to read its messages, collapse it, then expand a different session and see that session’s messages.

steps.description: Navigate to /sessions
Wait for the sessions list to finish loading
Click a session row to expand it
Wait for the message history to finish loading
Click the expanded session row to collapse it
Click a different session row to expand it
Wait for the message history to finish loading
Verify message history is displayed for the second expanded session

title: Export model cost data as CSV
description: Verifies that exporting from analytics triggers a CSV download that contains tabular model cost data for the selected period.

steps.description: Navigate to /analytics
Wait for the cost breakdown table to finish loading
Click the export action to download CSV
Verify a CSV file download is initiated
Verify the downloaded CSV contains rows consistent with the visible cost breakdown table

title: Edit and locally save project settings values
description: Verifies a user can change provider/model and edit settings fields then save and see the updated values reflected in the UI.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Select a different LLM provider value
Select a different model value
Fill a new value into the memory-related numeric field
Fill a new value into the compression-related numeric field
Fill a new value into the budget-related numeric field
Fill a new value into the privacy mask field
Click Save Changes
Verify the selected provider and model values remain set to the new selections
Verify the edited settings input fields display the newly entered values

title: Inspect SDK quick start sample
description: Verifies the overview page shows an SDK quick start sample so developers can copy the integration pattern.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Scroll to the SDK quick start section
Verify an SDK quick start code sample is displayed

title: Handle no usage data on the overview page
description: Verifies the overview page shows an empty state when there is no usage data available instead of failing or rendering broken charts.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Wait for the overview content to finish loading
Verify an empty state is displayed for usage and/or recent calls when no data is available

title: Reveal and hide the API key
description: Verifies a user can toggle API key visibility on and off and the UI updates accordingly.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Reveal the API key
Hide the API key
Verify the API key is not visible in clear text after hiding

title: Copy the API key to clipboard with confirmation
description: Verifies a user can copy the API key and sees an in-app confirmation that the copy action occurred.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Reveal the API key
Copy the API key
Verify a clipboard copy confirmation is visible

title: Show empty state when no calls are available
description: Verifies that when there are no logged calls, the debugger shows an empty state instead of a broken timeline.

steps.description: Navigate to /debugger
Wait for the timeline section to finish loading
Verify an empty state is visible for the call timeline

title: GDPR bulk delete blocks submission when user ID is empty
description: Verifies the GDPR bulk delete flow prevents deletion when the user ID field is empty and shows a blocking validation error.

steps.description: Navigate to /memory
Open the GDPR bulk delete flow
Submit the bulk delete form with the user ID field empty
Verify a user ID validation error is displayed and deletion does not proceed

title: Handle empty analytics state without breaking charts/table
description: Verifies that when analytics returns no data, the page shows an empty or zero-data state without broken UI.

steps.description: Navigate to /analytics
Wait for the analytics content to finish loading
Verify an empty or zero-data state is displayed for analytics visuals

title: Reject invalid numeric input in budget or memory fields
description: Verifies the UI prevents or flags invalid numeric input for numeric settings fields so the user cannot save nonsensical values.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Fill non-numeric text into a numeric settings field such as budget or memory limit
Click Save Changes
Verify a validation error is visible or the numeric field does not accept the invalid value

title: Settings reset after navigating away and returning
description: Verifies that settings changes do not persist after leaving the page and coming back, returning to default/initial values.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Select a different LLM provider value
Fill a new value into the budget-related numeric field
Click Save Changes
Click the navigation link to go to the overview dashboard
Click the navigation link to return to settings
Verify the provider selection has reset to its initial value
Verify the budget field has reset to its initial value

title: Settings reset after page reload
description: Verifies that settings changes do not persist after a browser reload and the settings return to initial values.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Select a different model value
Fill a new value into the memory-related numeric field
Click Save Changes
Reload the page
Verify the model selection has reset to its initial value
Verify the memory-related numeric field has reset to its initial value

title: Save changes with empty optional text privacy fields
description: Verifies a user can clear optional privacy text inputs and save without the UI getting stuck, and the cleared state is reflected.

steps.description: Navigate to /settings
Clear the privacy mask field
Click Save Changes
Verify the privacy mask field remains empty after saving
